sections.title: Introduction
sections.content:
Welcome to this comprehensive guide on using mddata, a powerful Python library for treating markdown files as structured data objects.

In this tutorial, you'll learn how to programmatically create, modify, and query markdown documents.

sections.title: Installation
sections.content:
Install mddata using uv:

```bash
uv add mddata
```

Or with pip:

```bash
pip install mddata
```

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
Here's a simple example to get you started:

```python
from mddata import MarkdownFile

# Load a document
doc = MarkdownFile('document.md')

# Access frontmatter
print(doc.mddata.title)

# Modify content
doc.mddata.set_section('intro', 'New introduction')

# Save changes
doc.save()
```

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.content: You now have the basics to start working with mddata. Check out the full documentation for more advanced features!
